reasoning: Dan Osborn officially formed an exploratory committee on April 3, 2025 to run against Senator Pete Ricketts in 2026. He quickly followed this up by releasing internal polling in mid-April showing him in a statistical tie, which even prompted Donald Trump to endorse Ricketts. This highly active posture strongly suggests he is building imminent momentum for a formal announcement. Typically, candidates transition from an exploratory committee to a formal campaign launch within a few weeks or months to begin raising 'hard' campaign dollars and capitalize on press attention. A launch before June 1, 2025 (within ~8 weeks of the exploratory announcement) is highly plausible and aligns with typical political timelines for high-profile challenges. However, Osborn does have a track record of delaying his timeline (he previously projected making his political decision in Nov/Dec 2024, then early March, before finally launching the exploratory committee in early April), which introduces a real chance that he could drag out the exploratory phase into the summer. Given the active polling and rapid political maneuvering, the probability of him formally announcing before June 1 leans favorably to a 'Yes' at 65%.
